Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An opaque solid cone is suspendended in the path of a parallel beam of light such that its shadow is cast on a screen oriented perpendicular to the direction of the light beam.
Under these conditions, which one of the shadows A, B, C and D is not possible?

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• A cone’s circular base can project a circle when the cone’s axis is perpendicular to the screen.
• Tilt the cone a bit and the circle becomes an ellipse.
• If the cone is oriented so the apex and base edge project on the screen, the outline can form a
triangle C the lateral surface projects as two straight sloping edges meeting at the apex.

Q8 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

The diameter of a sphere is decreased by 25%. By what percentage does its curved surface area decrease?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
If the diameter decreases by 25%, the radius also decreases by 25%.
So the new radius = 75% of the old radius = 0.75r
Curved surface area is proportional to the square of the radius : (0.75)2 = 0.5625
This means the new area is 56.25% of the original area
So, the decrease = 100 – 56.25 = 43.75%
Therefore, the curved surface area decreases by 43.75%.

Q12 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Given two tables R1(a, b) and R2(c, d) with 70 and 50 number of tuples respectively. Find maximum number of tuples in the output of natural join between two tables R1 and R2.

NAT
Correct answer: 3500
Solution

3500
• Natural join tests the equality condition between the relation using common attribute. If no
common attribute it gives cross product.
R1 has 70 tuple and R2 has 50 tuples.
So, max tuples will be 70 × 50 = 3500

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : The ReLU activation function is bounded above and has a finite maximum value.
S2 : The F1-score of a binary classifier attains its maximum value only when both precision and recall are equal to 1.
Which of the following is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• ReLU is defined as max(0, x) and is unbounded above; hence it has no finite maximum. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Since F1-score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall, it equals 1 if and only if both precision and recall are equal to 1. So, S2 is correct.

Q18 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Depth-limited search is a DFS variant, and DFS uses a stack (explicitly or via recursion) to
keep track of nodes. So, option (a) is correct.
• Plain DFS can get stuck in infinite branches; without a depth limit, it is not guaranteed to find
a solution. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• UCS requires a priority queue to always expand the lowest-cost node first. Using a stack
would break this property. So, option (c) is correct.
• UCS assumes non-negative step costs. Negative costs can cause cycles and UCS may fail to
terminate or find the correct solution. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q35 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a search tree with branching factor b and solution at depth d. Which statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• BFS stores all nodes at the current level; space grows exponentially with depth d. So, option
(a) is correct.
• DFS only stores nodes along the current path; space is Ο(b ⋅ d). So, option (b) is correct.
• Depth-limited search stores nodes along the path, so space is Ο(b ⋅ l), not Ο(bd). So, option (c) is incorrect.
• UCS stores all frontier nodes in a priority queue; space can be exponential in worst case.
So, option (d) is correct.

Q36 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1: It is preferable to use dense index rather than sparse index when the file is not sorted on the indexed field.
S2 : If index entries are sorted in ascending order, the occupancy of each leaf node of a B+ tree (except the last leaf node) is about 50%.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• It is preferable to use a dense index instead of sparse when the file is not sorted on index field
(such as when the index is a secondary index) or when the index file is small compared to the size of memory.
• If index entries are inserted in ascending order, the new entries get directed to the last leaf
node. When this leaf node gets filled, it is split into two. Of the two nodes generated by split,
the left node is left untouched and the insertions take place on the right node. This makes the
occupancy of the leaf nodes to about 50% except the last leaf.

Q39 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a relational database schema with two tables:
1. DEPARTMENTS
• DeptID (Primary Key, NOT NULL)
• DeptName
2. EMPLOYEES
• EmpID (Primary Key, NOT NULL)
• EmpName
• Mgr ID (Foreign Key referencing EmpID in EMPLOYEES)
• Dept ID (Foreign Key referencing DeptID in DEPARTMENTS)
Now, assume the following two specific constraints are applied to the foreign key on
EMPLOYEES.DeptID:
1. EMPLOYEES.DeptID is constrained as NOT NULL.
2. The foreign key definition includes the Referential Action: ON DELETE SET NULL.
A new department is created with DeptID = 100. An employee is hired and assigned to this
department: EmpID = 500, DeptID = 100.
Which of the following database operations will result in a Referential Integrity Error?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
1. The DELETE operation targets the department that is currently referenced by EmpID = 500.
2. The Foreign Key’s Referential Action is triggered: ON DELETE SET NULL.
3. The database attempts to automatically change the EMPLOYEES. DeptID value from 100 to NULL.
4. This attempt immediately violates the NOT NULL constraint explicitly placed on the
EMPLOYEES.DeptID column.
5. Because the database fails to execute the referential action, the original DELETE statement is
rolled back and an Referential Integrity Error is raised.
This operation tests the most advanced interaction: that a NOT NULL constraint on the referencing
column overrides an ON DELETE SET NULL action.

Q47 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : After splitting a decision tree node, the entropy value decreases
S2 : The feature chosen for splitting is the one that maximizes the decrease in entropy.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Splitting divides a node into subsets that are more “pure,” reducing disorder. This decrease
in entropy is the basis for information gain used in decision trees. So, S1 is correct.
• Decision tree algorithms select the feature with highest information gain. This ensures the
split reduces uncertainty the most, leading to a better tree. So, S2 is correct.

Q49 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding discretization of continuous attributes in data mining:
S1 : Supervised discretization uses class labels to determine interval boundaries, improving predictive performance.
S2 : Unsupervised equal-frequency discretization guarantees that all intervals have exactly the same width.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Supervised discretization uses the class labels to decide where to split the attribute values,
often improving classification accuracy. So, S1 is correct.
• Equal-frequency discretization divides the data so that each interval has approximately the
same number of data points, not equal width. Equal-width discretization divides the range
into intervals of equal width. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q52 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a Bayesian network converted into a junction tree. Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• Each junction tree node corresponds to a maximal clique in the moralized and triangulated
graph, which ensures consistent belief propagation across the network. So, option (a) is correct.
• Sampling on a junction tree is approximate; only full message passing guarantees exact inference. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Messages are passed between cliques using the sum-product algorithm, allowing computation of marginals efficiently. So, option (c) is correct.
• Junction tree sampling is not always faster than Gibbs sampling; efficiency depends on clique
sizes and network structure. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q64 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In Linear Discriminant Analysis, the optimal projection matrix W is obtained by maximizes

 
Which of the following statements is TRUE?
Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
• The objective function of LDA maximizes the ratio of between-class variance to within-class variance.
• This ensures that:
(i) Class means are pushed far apart (large SB).
(ii) Samples within each class are compact (small Sw).
Hence, LDA explicitly seeks projection directions that maximize class separability, which directly corresponds to option (c).
